name: Needs Simulation
description: A tick-based model where agents or player characters track needs such as hunger, rest, morale, hygiene, comfort, or safety and react to thresholds.
design_notes: Needs simulation is useful when the player can understand why an agent is thriving or failing. The design should define decay profiles, recovery sources, threshold effects, offline policy, and whether needs drive direct control, AI priorities, production, or colony health.
design_purpose: Create cause-and-effect pressure between resources, environment, schedules, and agent behavior.
Give management and survival games long-term problems that emerge from simple state changes.
Make agents feel persistent without requiring bespoke scripting for every condition.
player_fantasy: The player feels responsible for maintaining living systems whose needs react to their planning and neglect.

parameters.name: simulation_id
parameters.description: Simulation instance that owns need definitions and update policy.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Entity whose need state is being updated.

parameters.name: need_state_id
parameters.description: Specific need tracked by the simulation.

parameters.name: need_decay_profile_id
parameters.description: Decay profile selected from agent condition and environment.

parameters.name: recovery_source_id
parameters.description: Action, resource, or zone that restores the need.

parameters.name: threshold_effect_id
parameters.description: Effect triggered when the need crosses a threshold band.

parameters.name: simulation_tick_index
parameters.description: Ordered update index used for deterministic and saved updates.

parameters.name: offline_simulation_policy
parameters.description: Rule for applying needs while the game is not actively running.

edge_cases: An agent is asleep, injured, traveling, or in storage while needs continue to update.
A recovery source is consumed but the target need state changed before the transaction commits.
Offline progress would kill or permanently disable agents before the player can react.
Need thresholds trigger several events in one tick after a long pause or load.
Agents share a resource queue and all choose the same recovery source at once.
common_bugs: Needs update in render frames instead of simulation ticks and vary by frame rate.
Threshold effects fire repeatedly because the previous band is not stored.
Recovery consumes inventory client-side before the simulation accepts the state change.
Save snapshots store display bars but not raw need states or pending events.
Offline progress ignores capacity caps and drains every need to failure.
balancing_notes: Need decay should create planning pressure before it creates unavoidable failure.
More need categories increase systemic depth but also increase UI and cognitive load.
Offline decay should usually be capped or staged so players do not return to irreversible collapse.
accessibility_notes: Provide readable need labels, threshold warnings, and sortable agent lists.
Avoid requiring players to infer urgent needs from tiny icons alone.
Offer relaxed decay or pause-on-crisis settings for players who need more planning room.

implementation_notes.unity: Update needs from a simulation scheduler rather than MonoBehaviour frame updates. Store agent_id, need_state_id, previous threshold band, pending recovery transactions, and simulation_tick_index in save snapshots so reloads do not repeat events.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an autoload simulation manager or Resource-backed model for need updates. Run deterministic ticks, enqueue threshold events once per band crossing, and keep Control UI subscribed to committed state rather than editing need bars directly.
implementation_notes.unreal: A subsystem can own need state while Actors expose UI and behavior hooks. For networked or authoritative simulations, process recovery requests server-side and replicate threshold changes, not raw client-side guesses.
implementation_notes.web: Use a fixed-step reducer for need changes and serialize agent need records to local or server storage. Cap offline_simulation_policy results and include pending events in save_snapshot_id data for consistent resume behavior.

example_games.title: The Sims
example_games.note: Uses character needs to drive behavior and player decisions.

example_games.title: RimWorld
example_games.note: Uses colonist needs and moods as part of colony simulation.
